questions.question: What is a queue data structure, and what are its primary operations?
questions.hint: A queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure that allows elements to be added to the end and removed from the front. The primary operations are Enqueue, Dequeue, and Peek.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a circular queue and a double-ended queue.
questions.hint: A circular queue is a type of queue where the last element is connected to the first element, forming a circle. A double-ended queue, also known as a deque, is a type of queue that allows elements to be added or removed from both the front and rear ends.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of adding an element at the front of a queue, and what is it when removing an element from the front?
questions.hint: The time complexity of adding an element at the front of a queue is O(1), as it involves only shifting the existing elements. The time complexity of removing an element from the front of a queue is also O(1), as it involves only removing the front element.
